name: Helsing
overview: Helsing is a Munich-based defence-technology company, founded in 2021, that started as a 'software-first' AI firm and has since moved into building its own hardware. Its core idea is to put artificial intelligence at the centre of military systems — software that reads sensor data, helps make targeting decisions, and increasingly flies drones and aircraft. It is now one of Europe's most valuable startups, makes strike drones used in Ukraine, underwater sub-hunting drones for the UK, and AI that has flown a real Saab Gripen fighter jet.

established.label: Who they are
established.plain: Helsing was started in 2021 by Torsten Reil, Gundbert Scherf and Niklas Köhler, with Spotify founder Daniel Ek as an early backer and chairman. The pitch was simple: Europe needs its own AI for defence rather than relying on America. It began as a pure software company before later building its own drones and aircraft.

established.label: The brains tying weapons together
established.plain: Altra is Helsing's software platform that links sensors, drones, artillery and battlefield maps into one connected system. It lets a single human operator coordinate many drones at once — for example controlling a swarm of strike drones rather than flying each one by hand. It is the AI 'glue' that makes Helsing's hardware more than just individual gadgets.

established.label: An AI loitering munition
established.plain: Unveiled in December 2024, the HX-2 is an electrically-powered 'X-wing' attack drone (a loitering munition) with about 100 km range, built to hit tanks and structures. Onboard AI is meant to let it find and strike targets even when enemy jamming cuts the radio link, though Helsing says a human stays in control of the decision to attack. It is mass-produced at a factory in southern Germany.

established.label: Real combat deliveries
established.plain: Helsing first agreed in 2024 to co-produce 4,000 of its cheaper HF-1 strike drones with a Ukrainian partner, then announced in early 2025 a further 6,000 of the more advanced HX-2. This put Helsing's AI drones into actual frontline use, not just demos — and later exposed them to real battlefield scrutiny over how well they performed.

established.label: Europe's most valuable startup territory
established.plain: In June 2025 Helsing raised €600 million in a funding round that valued it at €12 billion, led by Daniel Ek's Prima Materia with backing from Lightspeed, Accel, General Catalyst and Saab. That valuation made it one of Europe's most valuable private companies and the standout name in the continent's defence-tech boom.

established.claim: Centaur flies a real Gripen fighter
established.label: AI in a real cockpit
established.plain: In May and June 2025, Helsing's AI agent 'Centaur' took control of a real Saab Gripen E fighter jet during test flights over the Baltic Sea, flying beyond-visual-range combat manoeuvres against another Gripen. A safety pilot could take back control at any time. It is widely reported as one of the first publicly known cases of AI flying a fully operational fighter jet.
established.sourceName: Saab — AI milestone with Gripen E

inProgress.claim: Cirra AI for Eurofighter electronic warfare
inProgress.label: AI to jam and detect threats
inProgress.plain: Germany picked Helsing's 'Cirra' AI software to go inside a new electronic-warfare version of the Eurofighter (the 'EK' variant) that will replace its retiring Tornado jets. Cirra is meant to spot, classify and respond to enemy radar and signals in real time, working with Saab's Arexis jamming system. The work runs over roughly three years with the upgrade targeted for completion around 2028.

inProgress.claim: SG-1 Fathom underwater drone for the UK
inProgress.label: AI sub-hunters
inProgress.plain: The SG-1 Fathom is a small autonomous underwater glider that can patrol silently for months, listening for submarines and protecting undersea cables and pipelines. The UK chose it as a core part of its 'Atlantic Bastion' undersea-defence programme, and Helsing has opened a factory in Plymouth to mass-produce the gliders for Britain and allies.

inProgress.label: A mass-producible AI war-plane
inProgress.plain: In September 2025 Helsing unveiled the CA-1 Europa, a clean-sheet uncrewed combat aircraft designed to be flown by its Centaur AI pilot, either alone or in swarms, for strike, reconnaissance and electronic-warfare missions. It is a design and ambition, not yet a flying aircraft: first flight is targeted for 2027 and service around 2029.

inProgress.label: European LLMs meet defence
inProgress.plain: In February 2025 Helsing teamed up with French AI firm Mistral to build 'vision-language-action' models for defence — AI that can understand what a platform is seeing, talk to operators in plain language, and help make faster decisions. It pairs Europe's leading large-language-model maker with its leading defence-AI maker, an explicitly sovereign, keep-it-in-Europe move.

latest.label: Battlefield reality check
latest.plain: In January 2026 Bloomberg reported that Ukraine and Germany had paused further HX-2 orders after the drones hit problems in combat — takeoff failures, missing AI features that had been promised, and losing the operator link under enemy jamming. Helsing publicly disputed the report, calling the main source 'unapproved and unverified' and saying Ukraine had ordered over 1,000 more units after successful frontline tests. The dispute is unresolved in public.
latest.sourceName: Bloomberg — Ukraine holds off on Helsing orders

latest.claim: Helsing disputes the Ukraine setback story
latest.label: The company's rebuttal
latest.plain: Helsing pushed back hard on the Bloomberg account, saying the HX-2 had been tested, approved for frontline use and that the Ukrainian army had placed orders for more than 1,000 additional drones. This is the company's side of a public disagreement about how its flagship drone is actually performing in the field.
latest.sourceName: The Defense Post — Helsing disputes report

suppliers.name: Saab
suppliers.role: partner + investor — Gripen AI flight (Centaur), Arexis EW suite paired with Cirra on Eurofighter; Series C/D investor
suppliers.claim: "This is an important achievement for Saab, demonstrating our qualitative edge in sophisticated technologies by making AI deliver in the air." — Peter Nilsson, Head of Advanced Programmes

suppliers.name: Mistral AI
suppliers.role: partner — joint vision-language-action AI models for defence
suppliers.claim: "With our partnership with Helsing, Europe is poised to take a significant step forward in asserting its technological leadership in AI." — Arthur Mensch, CEO

suppliers.name: Airbus Defence and Space
suppliers.role: partner / customer — Eurofighter EK programme integrating Cirra; earlier Wingman AI collaboration
suppliers.claim: "Manned-Unmanned Teaming will play a central role in achieving air superiority: with an unmanned Wingman at their side, fighter pilots can operate outside the danger zone." — Mike Schoellhorn, CEO
suppliers.sourceName: Airbus — collaboration with Helsing on AI

suppliers.name: HENSOLDT
suppliers.role: partner — joined Helsing on the CA-1 Europa autonomous combat aircraft
suppliers.claim: "The future of modern defence systems lies in the intelligent and networked interaction of sensor technology and artificial intelligence." — Oliver Dörre, CEO

suppliers.name: Ukrainian armed forces / industry
suppliers.role: customer — co-production and deliveries of HF-1 / HX-2 strike drones

suppliers.name: UK Ministry of Defence / Royal Navy
suppliers.role: customer — SG-1 Fathom underwater drones for the Atlantic Bastion undersea programme
suppliers.claim: "People should be in no doubt of the new threats facing the UK and our allies under the sea, where adversaries are targeting infrastructure so critical to our way of life." — John Healey

sources.name: Defense News — Saab/Helsing Gripen AI flight
Bloomberg — Ukraine holds off on Helsing orders

unknowns: How the HX-2 actually performs in combat is publicly disputed — Bloomberg's failure reporting and Helsing's rebuttal cannot be independently reconciled from open sources. Exact contract values, current order volumes, and revenue are mostly undisclosed, and the €12bn valuation reflects a later-2025/2026 reported raise toward an even higher figure that was not yet closed in public reporting.
